key_drivers: DXY remained below 99 and retained a weak short-term direction, supporting commodities and non-USD FX.
Cboe VIX stayed inside the 15-20 balanced regime and still fell on the day, which improved broad market tone without creating a full risk-off reading.
GDPNow held at 1.3%, preserving the slower-growth narrative that supports gold and parts of the index complex.
SOX stayed strong and copper turned constructive, while the MOVE index rose, creating a supportive but uneven pro-risk backdrop.

stale_sources: FRED real yields direction not freshly reconfirmed in this session
FRED high-yield credit-spread direction not freshly reconfirmed in this session
FRED 2s10s direction not freshly reconfirmed in this session
Oil supply and inventory classifications relied on retained prior context where no clean fresh official figure was readable
fallbacks_used: TradingView was used for DXY, MOVE, SOX, and copper directional confirmation
World Gold Council Weekly Markets Monitor was used to verify April gold ETF inflows
Fed stance was carried forward conservatively from the latest same-day verified repository run because CME FedWatch was blocked
overnight_changes: VIX at 19.12, down 0.57% on the day
DXY at 98.373 with a 5-day change of -0.69%
MOVE at 74.4185, up 3.14% on the day
SOX at 9,039.52, up 1.68% on the day
